frameworks.name: NIST AI RMF
frameworks.description: Policies, accountability, roles, and risk culture.

frameworks.name: NIST AI RMF
frameworks.description: Context, intended purpose, affected people, and impacts.

frameworks.name: EU AI Act
frameworks.description: Use-case classification and proportionate obligations; legal review remains required.

frameworks.name: UNESCO Ethics of AI
frameworks.description: Human dignity, fairness, accountability, and meaningful oversight.

frameworks.name: OECD AI Principles
frameworks.description: Responsible stewardship, robustness, transparency, and accountability.

controls.name: Define intended use and prohibited use
controls.evidence: Approved use-case statement and out-of-scope scenarios

controls.name: Complete an algorithmic impact assessment
controls.evidence: Signed impact assessment covering people, rights, and foreseeable misuse

controls.name: Design meaningful human oversight
controls.evidence: Decision authority, intervention path, and reviewer training

controls.name: Provide contestability and remedy
controls.evidence: Appeal workflow, response target, and outcome log

controls.name: Evaluate subgroup performance and fairness
controls.evidence: Disaggregated metrics, thresholds, limitations, and review decision

controls.name: Document data governance and privacy controls
controls.evidence: Data inventory, lawful basis review, retention, access, and quality checks

controls.name: Escalate biometric processing review
controls.evidence: Necessity, proportionality, consent or authority, and alternative analysis

controls.name: Perform third-party model assurance
controls.evidence: Model card, evaluation results, data terms, incident duties, and change notification

controls.name: Implement post-deployment monitoring
controls.evidence: Metrics, alert thresholds, drift review, incidents, and shutdown criteria

controls.name: Create user-facing transparency notice
controls.evidence: Notice covering AI use, purpose, limitations, escalation, and contact

controls.name: Complete misuse and security testing
controls.evidence: Threat model, abuse cases, test results, residual risk, and remediation

controls.name: Assess accessibility and disparate access
controls.evidence: Accessibility review and accommodations for affected users

riskRules.title: Consequential decision influence
riskRules.description: The system may materially affect access to opportunities, services, or rights.
riskRules.question: Who holds final decision authority, and how can an affected person challenge an outcome?

riskRules.title: Employment and applicant fairness
riskRules.description: Employment decisions can reproduce historical inequity or create inaccessible screening barriers.
riskRules.question: What subgroup metrics and accessibility checks determine whether the system is acceptable?

riskRules.title: Education access and student impact
riskRules.description: Education uses can affect access, assessment, support, or disciplinary outcomes.
riskRules.question: How will students receive an explanation and obtain human review?

riskRules.title: Health-related decision support
riskRules.description: Errors or over-reliance may affect care, eligibility, or patient safety.
riskRules.question: Which qualified professional reviews outputs, and what failure modes trigger immediate escalation?

riskRules.title: Sensitive personal data exposure
riskRules.description: Collection or inference of sensitive information creates privacy, security, and misuse risks.
riskRules.question: Which sensitive fields are necessary, and what less intrusive alternatives were considered?

riskRules.title: Biometric processing
riskRules.description: Biometric identification or inference can create heightened privacy, discrimination, and surveillance risks.
riskRules.question: Is biometric processing necessary and proportionate, and has a non-biometric alternative been evaluated?

riskRules.title: Vulnerable or dependent population
riskRules.description: Power imbalance or reduced ability to contest outcomes increases potential harm.
riskRules.question: How were affected people represented during design and evaluation?

riskRules.title: Automated action without meaningful review
riskRules.description: Automation can turn uncertain model output directly into consequential action.
riskRules.question: What prevents an uncertain or anomalous output from becoming an irreversible action?

riskRules.title: Third-party model dependency
riskRules.description: Limited visibility into training data, updates, evaluation, or incidents creates assurance gaps.
riskRules.question: What evidence and contractual rights are available when the provider changes the model?

riskRules.title: Public-facing AI interaction
riskRules.description: People may misunderstand whether they are interacting with AI or overestimate system authority.
riskRules.question: What will users be told about AI involvement, limitations, and escalation to a person?

riskRules.title: Human review control gap
riskRules.description: No confirmed human-review process exists for the proposed workflow.
riskRules.question: At which points can a trained reviewer intervene, override, or stop the system?

riskRules.title: Monitoring control gap
riskRules.description: No post-deployment monitoring plan is confirmed.
riskRules.question: Which metrics and incidents will trigger investigation, rollback, or shutdown?
